behaviors.description: The payload sent into summary generation contains fields from metrics, issue tracker, and deployment log.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: a unit test of the payload builder can pass while real source adapters are miswired or one adapter output is dropped during orchestration. REAL-INFRA is not required; local stubs for each source plus real orchestration code are enough to catch the wiring failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report assembly flow with three controlled source adapters (or local test doubles at module boundaries) returning distinct sentinel values. Assert the exact summary-input object includes all three sentinel datasets and correct section mapping.

behaviors.description: If one source is unavailable, the report still generates and includes a 'data unavailable' note for that section.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: pure unit tests can miss exception propagation and fallback behavior across collector -> assembler -> renderer boundaries. REAL-INFRA is not inherently required; injecting one failing adapter in an integrated run is sufficient to expose abort-vs-degrade bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Execute report generation with two sources returning data and one source raising a timeout/unavailable error. Assert process does not abort, output contains the unavailable marker in the correct section, and other sections are still populated.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary correctly reflects key values from the input data.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on model output quality (faithfulness to numeric/key facts), which can vary across runs/prompts/model versions. Mocking summary generation would hide real misrepresentation/hallucination failures.
behaviors.test_description: With fixed structured input containing known key values, call the real summary-generation model and evaluate factual consistency (e.g., required facts present, no contradictions, numeric values preserved within strict rules). Run repeatedly with acceptance threshold to capture nondeterministic failures.

behaviors.description: End-to-end report flow delivers a complete report email to configured recipients' inboxes.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this boundary includes real network/email transport and inbox receipt semantics. MOCK-HIDE: mocking mail delivery can miss authentication, connection, formatting, and provider acceptance failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full scheduled-report execution against real reachable test endpoints (or production-like staging services) and a real test mailbox. Assert message is received by configured recipient, includes expected report sections, and has valid subject/body formatting.

behaviors.description: If email service is unreachable, report is saved locally and retried successfully on next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: failure mode depends on actual transport unreachability plus filesystem persistence across runs and scheduler-triggered retry behavior. MOCK-HIDE: mocked mail clients often do not reproduce real connection/refusal/timeout semantics that drive fallback+retry paths.
behaviors.test_description: Run scheduled job with email endpoint intentionally unreachable (e.g., refused connection). Assert report artifact is written to local retry store. Trigger next scheduled run with email service restored and verify queued report is sent and marked cleared.
